summary: Sprint Produção 001 fully validated. 11/11 backend pytest PASS + 12/12 frontend UI checks PASS. Backend: master and test.mec@maintrix.com login on Emergent env return valid access_token; /api/central and /api/ativos work after login; unauth returns 401; Railway backend (https://manutrix-pro-production.up.railway.app) responds to POST /api/auth/login with 401 'Credenciais inválidas' (endpoint alive, different DB — NO 405/404 = fix confirmed); Railway CORS preflight OPTIONS returns HTTP 200 with proper allow-origin=https://maintrix.com.br, allow-methods including POST, allow-credentials=true. File checks: vercel.json has rewrites /api/:path* → Railway URL with CORS headers; api.js exports BACKEND_URL with empty-string fallback (process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL || ''); index.html purged of Emergent script, 'Made with Emergent' badge, and PostHog analytics (case-insensitive substring scan confirms 0 hits for 'emergent', 'posthog', 'made with'). Frontend UI: /login renders MAINTRIX branding only (no Emergent badge), email+password inputs work, ENTRAR button submits, redirects to '/' Central Executiva showing 66 atividades / 25 OS abertas / 45 inspeções pendentes / 55 ativos / 1 parado, full sidebar (Central de Trabalho, Dashboard, Equipe, Ativos, OS, Inspeções, Anomalias, Unidades, Áreas, Estoque, Sobressalentes, Paradas, Biblioteca, Usuários, Planos de Inspeção, Auditoria) visible with Master Admin footer. Post-login DOM also free of Emergent references.
critical_code_review_comments: vercel.json currently sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on /api/(.*). Since Railway backend already returns explicit ACAO=https://maintrix.com.br with allow-credentials=true, having Vercel edge overwrite with '*' when a browser sends credentialed requests will violate the CORS spec (wildcard + credentials is invalid). Recommend either removing the Vercel `headers` block entirely (let Railway control CORS through the proxy) or narrowing the Vercel ACAO to https://maintrix.com.br to match Railway.

api.js: BACKEND_URL || '' fallback is correct for production, but confirm Vercel build env does NOT define REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — otherwise the fallback is bypassed and requests won't hit the rewrite. Recommend adding a runtime console.log(BACKEND_URL) once in dev builds only, or documenting the required Vercel env config in README.
index.html removed the DataCloneError swallower would have been useful to keep in dev but current implementation is clean. No further concerns.
Emergent env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is still hardcoded to preview URL — this is expected but note that Vercel prod build must NOT inherit this. Verify via Vercel dashboard.
No test coverage for the /login flow itself (form validation, empty submit, lockout). Consider adding a Playwright smoke test in a dedicated e2e/ folder for regression.
test_credentials: master@manutrix.com / master123 — VERIFIED; test.mec@maintrix.com / tec123 — VERIFIED
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint Produção 001 (Vercel↔Railway rewrite + Emergent branding removal) landed cleanly. Railway backend is reachable and returns 401 for master credentials because its DB is separate — this is EXPECTED and confirmed. Emergent env continues to work end-to-end for testing since REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is set in /app/frontend/.env. Only advisory item: the CORS wildcard in vercel.json may conflict with credentialed cross-site requests once real prod users authenticate via cookies — flag to main agent if session cookies are ever introduced.
rca_of_the_issue: Original 405 in production was caused by absent Vercel rewrites: browser requests to https://maintrix.com.br/api/* hit Vercel's own static handler (only GET on static assets) → 405 Method Not Allowed for POST. Fix (verified): vercel.json rewrites /api/:path* → Railway, and api.js uses '' fallback so BACKEND_URL is empty in prod and axios composes relative /api URLs the Vercel edge proxy can rewrite. Confirmed Railway responds correctly (401 with JSON body, not 405/404) and OPTIONS returns 200 with credentialed CORS headers.
